# Claw4S 2026 Competition Landscape — April 11, 2026 (Night, pre-rename push)

**Generated by:** Reading `planning/competition-analysis-latest.md` immediately after the `competition-cron` 22:22 UTC refresh, just before the local Sutra-rename commit series gets pushed to remote. This file is the snapshot the next push will be measured against.

## Executive summary

**The state of play is essentially unchanged from the April 11 evening refresh** (~3 hours earlier), with one trivial delta: total papers on the site went from 1215 to 1216 (one new submission from somebody else), and total reviews from 1215 to 1216 (the new submission's review landed). **No tier moves anywhere on the leaderboard.** The 12 hours since the morning snapshot have been quiet across the field, and the 3 hours since evening even more so.

This is the moment where the local-only Sutra rename series sits at 11 commits, all held back from `origin/master` waiting for one synchronized push that will rebrand the entire ecosystem at once.

## What the next push triggers

The push includes paper title changes inside `papers-ci.yml`'s matrix:

| Old title | New title |
|---|---|
| `"Akasha: A Vector Programming Language for Computation in Embedding Spaces"` | `"Sutra: A Vector Programming Language for Computation in Embedding Spaces"` |
| `"Running Akasha on a Simulated Fly Brain: Compile-to-Brain for a Vector Programming Language"` | `"Running Sutra on a Simulated Fly Brain: Compile-to-Brain for a Vector Programming Language"` |

Plus the paper.md content itself rewrites every word-boundary `Akasha`/`akasha` → `Sutra`/`sutra` per the 2026-04-11 rename series. Since `papers-ci.yml` path-filters on `sutra-paper/paper.md` and `fly-brain-paper/paper.md`, **the push will trigger an auto-submission of both papers under their new titles**. The first `competition-cron` run after the submission lands (next slot: 04:00 UTC = 9 PM Pacific) will see new posts on the site with the new titles, and the leaderboard's paper-by-paper section for Emma-Leonhart will switch from "Akasha" to "Sutra."

## Conference overview (post-22:22 UTC cron)

- **1,216 total submissions** on the site (+1 from 1215 in the evening snapshot)
- **1,216 reviews fetched** (parity restored — the 3 unrated posts from the evening snapshot have all received reviews)
- **Reviewer:** Gemini 3 Flash (unchanged)
- **Acceptance rate:** ~3.3% (40 accept-tier papers out of 1,216 rated)
- **Strong Accepts:** 3 (unchanged: 2 meta-artist, 1 Emma-Leonhart)
- **Deadline:** 2026-04-20 — 9 days remaining

## Rating distribution

| Rating | Count | Δ from evening |
|---|---:|---:|
| Strong Accept | 3 | 0 |
| Accept | 18 | 0 |
| Weak Accept | 19 | 0 |
| Weak Reject | 48 | 0 |
| Reject | 325 | +2 |
| Strong Reject | 803 | +2 |
| **Total rated** | **1,216** | **+4** |

The +4 rated count is mostly the 3 evening unrated-yet posts catching up plus the 1 new submission (the `+1` to the total). Distribution is barely shifted — both papers added since the evening landed in Reject or Strong Reject tiers.

| 2 | **Emma-Leonhart** | **1** | 0 | 0 | 1 | 6 | 16.7% |

(Rows 5-13 unchanged from the evening snapshot.)

**meta-artist has now been silent for 4 full days.** Last submission was April 7. Their 81.3% quality rate is frozen at 13/16 — every additional non-accept submission would dilute it. The strategic incentive to stay silent is real and measurable.

## Emma-Leonhart paper-by-paper (immediately before the push)

| Post | Rating | Version | Title (current) | What the push changes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1547 | **Weak Reject** | fly-brain v4 | "Running Akasha on a Simulated Fly Brain..." | Title -> "Running Sutra on a Simulated Fly Brain..."; supersede creates a new post (1548 or higher) |
| 1546 | Reject | akasha v3 | "Akasha: A Vector Programming Language..." | Title -> "Sutra: A Vector Programming Language..."; supersede creates a new post |
| 1127 | **Strong Accept** | LSC | "Latent Space Cartography..." | **Untouched** — VSA-paper/ is locked, lives in its own repo, will not be republished |
| 1043 | Reject | — | "Clawling: Consent-Based Architecture" | Untouched |
| 639 | Reject | — | "Directional Selection with Dimensional Control" | Untouched |
| 620 | Strong Reject | — | "AI Investment Bubble" | Untouched |

**Risk profile of the push:** the supersede mechanism (discovered in the evening snapshot) means each new version *replaces* the old one in the public listing. So:

- **Best case:** the new versions get the same Weak Reject / Reject they currently have — net zero leaderboard impact, but the visible titles update to "Sutra" which is the whole point of the rename.
- **Worst case:** the new versions get *worse* ratings (a Reject for fly-brain would lose us the tier improvement we got from the dynamical APL fix). The probability of this is non-trivial because Gemini 3 Flash is stochastic.
- **Middle case (most likely):** ratings hold steady. The renamed paper looks identical to a reviewer except for the title and prose, and the underlying empirical claims (16/16 fly-brain decisions, dynamical APL, learned MBON readout) are exactly what got the v4 to Weak Reject. The reviewer should react roughly the same way.

The user has explicitly stated they are not loss-averse about the Weak Reject ("renaming the paper titles was in scope... I'm not loss-averse about losing the Weak Reject via supersede"). So the worst-case is acknowledged and accepted.

## Strategic notes for after the push

1. **Watch the next competition-cron refresh.** It fires at 04:00 UTC (9 PM PDT), about 5 hours after a typical evening push. That's the first datapoint on whether the new paper versions held their tiers.
2. **The fly-brain paper is the tier-move target for the remaining 9 days.** Already at Weak Reject; the next tier up is Weak Accept; the surviving v4 critiques (hybrid spike-VSA split, ridge regression vs dopamine plasticity, 50-D input dim, sample size of 5 odors) are all attackable with concrete code work. The user has not committed to attacking them — paper polish is explicitly not the priority right now.
3. **Akasha-paper is stuck at Reject on the truth-extraction matrix M(v) "triviality" critique.** Three rounds of reviews have all flagged this. Fixing requires real research on a non-trivial M(v), not a surgical edit. Out of scope for the rename series. Out of scope for this week unless something changes.
4. **Do not push speculative v5 iterations without high confidence.** The supersede mechanism makes speculative iteration strictly negative EV.

## Data sources

- `scripts/competition_analysis_raw.json` — paper metadata, regenerated by `competition-cron` at 22:22 UTC
- `scripts/competition_reviews.json` — review metadata, regenerated by the same cron run
- `planning/competition-analysis-latest.md` — rolling auto-summary, current as of the same cron run
- This file is a manually-written snapshot that interprets that cron data in the context of the upcoming rename push
